steps.title: Modify the lens subject
steps.content: The sentence already has the main verb "ensured." The words in the blank should describe what the plano-convex lens was doing to the laser while leaving "ensured" as the only finite verb in the clause.

steps.title: Reject extra main verbs
steps.content: A and B are finite verb forms, so each would create an uncoordinated sequence in which the lens both focuses the laser and ensured the spot size. D is also a bare verb form that cannot smoothly modify the subject here. The participle form creates a modifying phrase before the main verb. The answer is C.
